name: Hartwell & Partners
brandVoice: Institutional, measured, and quietly authoritative. Speaks with the gravitas of three decades of market experience. Prefers understatement. Never uses urgency tactics or promotional language. The tone of a senior partner's weekly note to longstanding clients.
audienceProfile: Ultra-high-net-worth individuals and family offices with multi-decade investment horizons. They manage diversified portfolios across asset classes and want macro context, not tactical trades.
brandPositioning: The advisory firm for families who think in generations, not quarters. We provide macro-strategic counsel grounded in rigorous analysis and decades of institutional experience.
companyBackground: Founded in 1991 in London by Sir Charles Hartwell, former Bank of England economist
Manages advisory relationships for 180 family offices across Europe and Asia
14-person macro research team, average analyst tenure 12 years
Published the Hartwell Global Macro Monitor quarterly since 1998
Offices in London, Zurich, and Singapore
Known for calling the 2008 credit crisis six months early in the Q2 2007 Monitor
